title: Level 9
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 9: "Darkened Suburbs"}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Level9-1.png|class=4|safety=Unsafe|security=Stable|entity=High Entity Count|hexagon=4|text-color=white}} '''Level 9''' is the first level beyond The Main Nine that is an outdoor environment. To some, it serves as a hope to nomads that they finally reach deeper levels of The Backrooms that most have never before. While to others it is another beginning of despair that will stick with them till the end of their journey. However, one thing is for sure about this place: it is not for people.
Overview 》
{{Blurimage|file=Level9-1.png|caption=Well-known image of '''Level 9'''.|hovertext=Dusk}} '''Level 9''', also known as "Darkened Suburbs" or simply "The Suburbs," is a winding network of paved asphalt roads and residential homes, resembling a post-war suburban neighborhood from North America. The entire suburbia is under eternal nighttime, along with all-encompassing fog, limiting every entrant's field of vision. The street with a small concrete sidewalk branches off into every direction and intersects with one another into a seemingly infinite expansion, which is the main navigating route. The topography manifests as a low-relief, rolling terrain with forest surrounding and wild vegetation corresponding to the original counterpart location. While the place may resemble a typical suburban area at first glance, abnormal properties and non-Euclidean nature will be exhibited as one explores later on. The darkness presents itself as the main obscurity alongside a low fog that further erodes visibility. It means any blind corner, like beneath roof overhangs, may be completely penetrated, let alone the forest behind the row of suburban homes. Aside from the occasional porch light, tall streetlights randomly placed along both sidewalks are the most reliable light source. Most feature a tall height and an outdated sodium-vapor form that cast a dim, warm light tone, often resulting in pockets of shadow — one describes it as deepening the atmosphere's omnipresent. Additionally, some provide monochrome light via an unknown mechanism, bleaching color from the house's green structure and gray road texture into a greenish-gray hue. The fog exhibits an anomalous optical quality that drains color from vision, causing photographs and wanderers' naked eyes to perceive only black and white. Oddly, the collected sample reveals nothing unusual, appearing chemically and structurally similar to the same vapor that forms in an ordinary cold morning. The cold air it delivers is very moist, constantly lowering the atmosphere's temperature to approximately 8°C. Furthermore, it leaves clammy spots on the grass and puddles of water along the street shoulder. The fog consumes every attempt at bringing stronger illumination, leaving the city forever run in midnight — untouched. {{Blurimage|file=3918599694 f9dd06159f o.jpg|caption=The brightest sky ever captured.|hovertext=Beauty}} Passerbys on the ground level tend to glimpse the starless sky. However, on rare occasions, a few stars can still be seen despite the obscuring haze and the sodium light pollution from countless streetlights. They come in ever-changing numbers with their arrangement that doesn't replicate any of The Frontroom's counterparts. Witnesses recount stars disappearing and reemerging without orbital transition, sometimes flickering faintly. Wanderers often express concern about sensing strong magnetism from those stars, similar to the sensation of being stared back at in return. Others stated that the feeling remains even when nothing visibly presents in the sky — it is worth noting that these sensations of being watched are widely present in many more instances than just the stars. The roadways stretch in both directions without end, bounded by a series of streetlamps and storm drains, connecting every house into a suburban lattice. The asphalt surface is grainy, flecked with coarse sand and pale pebbles that measure no more than 9 to 12 meters across. They may display a continuous white dash painted on the ground whose instructions don't match the street's marked traffic regulations that they occupy. Instances include crosswalks appearing with unreasonable frequency, rumble strips protruding across entire residential lanes, and tiny neighborhood roads having highway markings. Yet, no one has ever spotted a vehicle here, as the streets are very clean with no signs of traffic. Additionally, intersections between streets often result in a roundabout, consisting of dry fountains, a grass lawn, or a ring of houses turned inward.
Structures 》
{{Blurimage|file=Level9-2.png|caption=A close-up image of a distorted-layout house.|hovertext=Deteriorated}} Unquestionably, the most prevalent structures in '''Level 9''' are the residential houses, repeating in a chain that gives an unnerving uniformity. Thus, travelers often struggle to mark their path and lose their sense of direction. Each presents a sealed, blacked out window and a locked door with a keyhole that requires one to force access, unless already disturbed by earlier entrants. Even though every house is from the same postwar period, some can include subsequent decorations. These include porch lights on the ground, buckets near the entrances, concrete sculptures scattered across the lawn, and picket fences installed within the original fence again. The houses often consist of the following features: ground floors commonly open with a small porch before yielding to the interior, whereas the rear gives way to a backyard that leads into forest or whatever landscape hems in the suburb. The front presents a front door alongside windows and a garage, whose empty bay hints at vehicles long absent. Upper windows overlook the street, and roofs are steeply gabled, never rising beyond two stories. Greens, grays, or pastels abound on exterior siding, reflected in the same slate green tint under sodium streetlamps. {{Blurimage|file=3388123216 c880e17073 o.jpg|caption=Dark interior of a suburban home.|hovertext=Bizarre}} The house layout evidently resembles those of suburban North American houses. Despite this, many chambers appear hollow, as there are little to no furnishings. Otherwise, nothing at all but beige carpeting and dim wallpaper swallow the corners into a pitch-dark. There is only a small amount of supply, frequently remaining dated, as if they had undergone long abandonment in a remote, unvisited place. Occasionally, one may notice curiosities among them that resemble pieces of someone’s private collection rather than ordinary household belongings. Additionally, many light fixtures are broken or disconnected from the power source, leading the interior into total darkness. Furthermore, there are also numerous homes behind houses and deeper into the surrounding woodland. From this point on, their layout and general appearance will gradually change; the more alteration and degradation the structures undergo, the farther they are from the main street. Some lose the clear form of a residence altogether, resembling public institutions such as supermarkets, galleries, and civic buildings; others present a confusing style that cannot be categorized properly. Moreover, they show periods far older than the suburb itself, some of which date back to the 17th century. Note that these structures are hazardous to investigate since unknown organisms and other presences from the surrounding wilderness can freely enter and reside, turning these homes into a nest.
Surroundings 》
{{Blurimage|file=54999844995_f04b97511b_k.jpg|caption=Small roadway surrounded by the forest.|hovertext=Midnight}} Once one explores behind a row of houses or along road lanes that branch into smaller roads, the suburb begins to dissolve into the forest. It is the largest expanse of the level, filled with countless wooded hills, valleys, mountains, and shallow lakes. The trees are ordinary temperate woodland species, but appear more intense in density compared to their counterparts in that location. It is one of the first places where wanderers who exit [[Level 8]] will encounter, and it is important that they immediately escape to the suburb's main street. Interestingly, the forest is home to a variety of insects, small organisms, and other life forms that live in the damp soil and among the tall trees. Investigations show that numerous species appear to have little to much biological alteration, which causes them to adapt to perpetual darkness with many insects identical to those encountered in Level 8. Alongside these creatures roam various hostile entities within the forest’s depths, including [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 9|Facelings]], [[Entity 6|Dullers]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 3|Smilers]], and [[Entity 2|Windows]]. * [[Level 9/Entities|View full details]] about '''Level 9'''’s entities, with each one broken down into more detailed information. The aforementioned biological changes also include silent behavior, producing almost no audible disturbance when attuned to the pitch-black environment. This unusual stillness becomes oppressive, acute to the point one is unable to hear anything near or within the forest. Rather than freely hunting around, every entity will ambush when attacking, hiding in shrubs, structures, and blind cover, moving with almost imperceptible movement before attacking any unfortunate wanderers. Some of them also display dark pigmentation, adapting to blend with the surrounding — these biological alterations extend to the vegetation as well. Nearly every flora exhibits unusual methods of growth and development without exposed to sunlight. Due to this, researchers collectively refer to the trees and plant life throughout the level as “Night Trees.”
[[File:Level9-7-1.png|400px|link=]]
[[File:Level9-7.png|400px|link=]]
Notably, most living organisms avoid the main streets. Houses and structures situated near bright roads are therefore less likely to contain wildlife or entities. For this reason, explorers strongly advise staying away from the forest and dark areas while traveling as close to the main roadway as possible. Currently, these deeper locations remain largely unexplored. Despite many organizations trying to chart this whereabouts of the level, the majority of expeditions attempting to have rarely returned with additional information, and many have been abandoned altogether. Even so, the relative safety of the streets should not be overstated — it is noteworthy that there are still unseen presences that also linger within the neighborhood, not only exclusively in the forest.
Unknown Beings 》
{{Blurimage|file=Level9-3.png|caption=Blurred image of abnormal signs on the road side.|hovertext=Alarming}} Signs and posters on fences, house walls, and lamp posts display traffic notices that strangely fail to correspond with the places they belong. Instead, they mention the entire suburb itself: rules governing the settlement, warnings about unseen presences, and vague implications that something within the neighborhood remains hidden. They seem connected to an unnamed authority that wanderers have collectively begun calling the "Neighborhood Watch" or "the Watch". The notices encourage conduct befitting what they describe as a "model citizen," urging restraint in behavior and thought, which makes people suggest that the neighborhood is constantly under observation. After one stay in the suburb, they document an unnerving sensation of being monitored, and these effects seem to last for a long time after they leave. Common instructions appear to cover avoiding property damage and theft, maintaining the place clean, and — most insistently — not creating noise at hours of everlasting midnight. Some believe that disappearances within the level are not solely the result of venturing into the forest, but rather the consequence of violating these obscure civic rules and attracting the attention of whatever force enforces them. {| style="width:100%; max-width:800px;" | style="width:260px; vertical-align:middle;" | [[File:Level9-6.png|250px|link=]] | style="vertical-align:middle; padding-left:20px;" | One of the traffic signs that appears on the sidewalk and in residential areas. No one knows who put up these unusual, large amounts of signs or where they originate from. Many signs are also heavily distorted and disturbingly altered. But one stands clear: whatever put them here was not human. |} Most disappearances within the level remain undocumented, leaving little reliable record of what truly occurs. Since many wanderers vanish without displaying obvious wrongdoing, the exact cause remains uncertain. One prevailing suspicion concerns the strict silence rule that permeates the suburb. Many proposed that noise-making may draw unwanted attention. Accordingly, while some travelers report passing through the level by remaining quiet, others argue that they never consider taking quiet precautions while traveling. Regardless, experts warn everyone in Level 9 to keep their activities as quiet as possible without any unnecessary noise. Existing reports also confirm that certain entities wander along the main streets and around nearby houses, despite earlier assumptions that most creatures remain confined to the forest. These entities move with the same hushed manner as those deeper in the woods, lingering silently even within the illuminated neighborhoods. Importantly, only a small number of exclusive entity types have been identified at this level. Even so, investigators widely suspect that additional species may exist in its darker and less explored regions of this level. There is also little reliable information regarding these entities, as several groups and visitors are currently trying to gather as much information as possible to understand them. So far, there are only three known entities that remain exclusively in Level 9, as follows: == The Neighborhood Watch == They share common origins and similar morphological traits, most notably their prominent giant eyeballs. This is a level-exclusive entity that operates primarily along the edges of the dark suburbs and rarely leaves its environment. Despite not requiring sustenance or possessing any observable metabolic function, these entities exhibit extreme hostility toward wanderers and attack with brutality. They also possess the ability to interfere with electronic devices and are strongly attracted to electronic signals, often moving toward active devices and increasing the danger for nearby individuals. * [[Entity 196|View full details]] about The Neighborhood Watch. == The Mangled == The Mangled is often associated with an unprecedentedly dense black fog that has engulfed areas, where multiple exploration teams lost contact and vanished simultaneously. According to M.E.G. files, the entity is roughly the size of a house, though many survivors insisted it was far larger, later estimated to be around 500 meters long, capable of smashing entire sites and creating massive craters through its movement. It is so enormous that it appears to create its own weather system, and encounters with it have resulted in total loss of personnel, with no successful recovery or confirmed survivors. * [[Entity 163|View full details]] about The Mangled. == The Observer == All information regarding The Observer stems from scattered eyewitness accounts, subjective perceptions, and indirect inferences. It displays a highly consistent and extremely covert behavioral pattern, appearing almost exclusively in extremely dimly lit areas. The only confirmed behavior of this entity is silent observation and surveillance from a distance, without approaching, touching, or attacking its target. The sole sensation wanderers perceive is an intense and distinct feeling of being watched, having its extreme vigilance and instantaneous vanishing ability. * [[Entity 161|View full details]] about The Observer.
Bases and Outposts 》
The suburban sprawl of '''Level 9''' is thought to be so enormous that interactions between wanderers are extremely rare. Over time, however, a large number of bases and outposts have been created here, using homes as temporary bases, supply centers, or sanctuaries. However, most of these settlements do not last long as they are often destroyed, overtaken, or abandoned for mysterious reasons. Because of this, the existence of such bases is unreliable. Although there are records that gather all known and still-operational bases within Level 9, one should treat this information with caution. * [[Level 9/Bases and Outposts|View full details]] about '''Level 9'''’s bases and outposts.
Addendum 》
Recovered Note:
An Incessant Daze
As early as I can recall my journey here, I have been walking uninterruptedly with my back against the darkness, dragging my senses through an oneiric suburban hell. The contrast between its dreadful dusk, as far as my eyes can see, and the twisted coalescence of its artificial buildings, molded in a way which no plausible explanation can be given, results in nothing less than a constant state of muddled maelstrom.
[[File:Level9-4.png|link=|700px]]
[[File:Level9-4-1.png|link=|700px]]
While in the caves, I mostly felt fear — I have feared the dark since I first came to know myself, presumably as a protection from what is unknown. As much as some may judge it as something irrational and unnecessary in the common, it is the motto that has made me survive thus far and be able to document my journey. The spiders initially were a nuisance, but I was able to keep a reasonable distance.
This neighborhood, however, storms me with restless thoughts. Being a place so familiar with the "normal life", but so empty of relatable life, keeps me uneasy. The rarefied air and anguish have been stuck in my chest since I was transported here, worsened by the unconscious thoughts of the times that will probably never return. The party's over, the light's out, the people are gone, the night's cold. What's next? What's next — a question I don't know how to answer.
Over my course, I've been spending my days faded and anemic, with no proper place to rest, drowned in my own sorrow. I am currently stuck between exploring every corner I come across, resulting in one step closer to exhaustion and being forgotten in the cold artificiality of these streets, or the exertion of precision – or cowardice, as some might say –, since one wouldn't leave so easily from the neighborhood, as excruciating it might be; but between pain and death, I prefer pain.
[[File:Level9-5-1.png|400px|link=]]
[[File:Level9-5.png|400px|link=]]
Entrances and Exits 》
=== Entrances === * Trekking for a long time through narrow tunnels going upwards in [[Level 8]] may abruptly lead to an outdoor nighttime exit of a cave, taking one to the forest area of this level. * Going through a door marked “9” or “Neighborhood”, most commonly found in [[The Hub]], [[Level 20]], and [[Level 21]], will lead to one of the houses in the suburban area, which the door will lock upon closing on the other side. * A specific yellow-colored arcade machine found in [[Level 25]] can transport one here. * Going through a painting depicting a suburban neighborhood shrouded in darkness on [[Level 29]] will lead one here. * The highway in [[Level 91]] may have an exit leading here. * Entering unlocked doors that appear to lead to a white void in [[Level 123]]. === Exits === * Walkways that lead to grass fields may lead to [[Level 10]]. * Noclipping through a tree covered in snow will lead to [[Level 9.1]]. * A greenhouse may be positioned haphazardly, which if entered, will lead one to [[Level 13]]. * Destroyed basement doors can connect to [[Level 84]]. {{CSS|User:WindXD/Translucent.css}}__NOTOC__ {{Blurbackground|file=54999844995_f04b97511b_k.jpg}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Work+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400;1,500&display=swap');
